label: M1_DCM-SchemaRegistry
info:
Backend flows for Schema Registry Management.

════════════════════════════════════════════════
  HOW TO SET YOUR OPENAI API KEY
════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. Double-click this flow tab name (DCM-SchemaRegistry)
2. In the Edit Flow panel, click the 'Environment Variables' section
3. Find the variable named: OPENAI_API_KEY
4. Replace PUT_OPENAI_API_KEY_HERE with your real OpenAI API key
   (e.g. sk-proj-abc123...)
5. Click Done, then Deploy

All OpenAI request nodes in this flow use this single key.
Do NOT paste the key anywhere else.

════════════════════════════════════════════════

OpenAI calls use real http request nodes — not simulated.

MongoDB collections used:
  sr_prompts        – prompt records
  sr_test_cases     – saved test cases
  sr_llm_configs    – LLM configuration records
  sr_providers      – multi-model provider records
  sr_local_schemas  – local schema definitions
  sr_mappings       – schema mapping configurations

All responses are sent back to the Dashboard via link-out → dashboard-linkin.

name: Build Enhance Request Payload

Parse Enhance Error
── 2. CREATE PROMPT + CODE GENERATION (real OpenAI POST) ─
Build Prompt Record for Insert
Send Immediate "writing code" Response
Build Code-Gen Request Payload
Extract Generated Code (success)
Handle Code-Gen HTTP Error
Prepare DB Update (code + status)
Send "codeGenerated" Response to UI
Prepare Find All Prompts
Format listPrompts Response
Prepare updateOne (code)
Format updatePromptCode Response
Prepare deleteOne (prompt)
Format deletePrompt Response
Prepare findOne (prompt for dry run)
Execute Transform Function
Prepare Test Case insert/update
Format saveTestCase Response
Prepare Find All Test Cases
Format listTestCases Response
Prepare deleteOne (test case)
Format deleteTestCase Response
── 10. UPDATE PROMPT (full metadata edit + conflict check) ─
Check if Active Conflict Query Needed
Evaluate Conflict (updatePrompt)
Prepare updateOne (full prompt)
Format updatePrompt Response
Check if Active Status Conflict Needed
Evaluate Conflict (updateStatus)
Prepare updateOne (status)
Format updatePromptStatus Response
Prepare LLM Config insert/update
Format saveLlmConfig Response
Prepare deleteOne (llm config)
Format deleteLlmConfig Response
Prepare Provider insert/update
Format saveProvider Response
Prepare deleteOne (provider)
Format deleteProvider Response
Prepare Find All Providers (for reorder)
Reorder + Format Response
Batch Retransform (simulated processing)
Cancel Batch Retransform
Prepare Find All LLM Configs
Format listLlmConfigs Response
Prepare Find All Providers
Format listProviders Response
Prepare Local Schema insert/update
Format saveLocalSchema Response
Prepare Find All Local Schemas
Format listLocalSchemas Response
Prepare deleteOne (local schema)
Format deleteLocalSchema Response
Prepare Mapping insert/update
Format saveMapping Response
Prepare Find All Mappings
Format listMappings Response
Prepare deleteOne (mapping)
Format deleteMapping Response
══ SETUP: Double-click the flow tab → set OPENAI_API_KEY env var ══
info:
WHERE TO PUT YOUR OPENAI API KEY:

1. Double-click the tab name 'DCM-SchemaRegistry' at the top
2. Open 'Environment Variables'
3. Replace PUT_OPENAI_API_KEY_HERE with your real key
4. Click Done → Deploy

All OpenAI-dependent operations (enhance prompt, code generation)
use real http request nodes that POST to
https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions.

Function nodes are used ONLY for:
  – payload shaping / request preparation
  – response parsing
  – validation and status updates
  – MongoDB query preparation
  – dry-run code execution

name: ▶ Store OpenAI Config in Flow Context
